slides.title: The minute after exercise *says the most*.
slides.sub: How fast your pulse drops once you stop is a quiet window into how long you may live.

slides.title: Heart-rate recovery
slides.body: It is how many beats your pulse falls in the first minute after hard effort. A *quick drop* is a good sign.

slides.title: A window into the nerves
slides.body: That fast slowdown reflects vagal tone, the calming branch of your nervous system. Stronger tone *can support* steadier health.

slides.title: One of the strongest signals
slides.body: A slow recovery in that first minute *is linked to* higher long-term risk in large studies. The drop is telling.

slides.title: Count the first minute
slides.body: Note your peak rate, rest a minute, then check again. A larger fall *generally points to* better fitness.

slides.title: Train, sleep, recover
slides.body: Regular cardio, good sleep and real recovery days *can improve* how quickly your heart settles over time.

slides.title: Watch the signal *climb*.
slides.body: Vibe Health brings your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so the habits behind a fitter heart are easy to see.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The minute after you stop training is one of the most honest health checks you have. 🫀🌿

A quick pulse drop reflects strong vagal tone, and it is one of the clearest signals of a long, capable life. Build it with cardio, sleep and real recovery. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Heart-Rate Recovery: A Quiet Predictor of a Long Life
